courses.title: IELTS Reading: Essential Strategies
courses.description: Master core reading skills to achieve Band 6.5+. This comprehensive course covers Skimming, Scanning, and how to effectively tackle True/False/Not Given and Matching Headings questions under time pressure.

courses.title: IELTS Listening Advanced
courses.description: Nâng cao kỹ năng Listening với các bài tập thực tế.

courses.title: IELTS Writing Task 2 Masterclass
courses.description: Chinh phục Writing Task 2 với chiến lược bài bản.

courses.title: IELTS Listening Foundation (Dành cho người mới bắt đầu)
courses.description: Khóa học nền tảng giúp học viên làm quen với các format câu hỏi cơ bản trong bài thi IELTS Listening như Multiple Choice và Form Completion. Bao gồm 10 bài nghe thực tế tốc độ chậm.

courses.title: Master IELTS Writing Task 2 - Band 7.0+
courses.description: Khóa học nâng cao cung cấp bộ template cấu trúc bài viết, cách triển khai ý tưởng (brainstorming) và từ vựng học thuật ăn điểm cho phần thi Writing Task 2. Chấm chữa 5 bài Essay.

courses.title: IELTS Reading: Skimming & Scanning Techniques
courses.description: Tập trung rèn luyện hai kỹ năng quan trọng nhất trong IELTS Reading: Đọc lướt tìm ý chính và đọc quét tìm từ khóa. Bao gồm 15 bài Test mô phỏng từ kho đề Cambridge.

courses.title: Confident IELTS Speaking Part 1 & 2
courses.description: Khóa học giúp phá vỡ rào cản ngôn ngữ, luyện phản xạ nói tự nhiên, chữa ngọng và điều chỉnh phát âm (Pronunciation). Bao gồm tài liệu từ vựng theo 20 chủ đề thường gặp nhất.

courses.title: IELTS Reading Beginner Course
courses.description:
This course helps students improve IELTS reading skills from band 4 to 6.
skill: Reading

courses.title: IELTS Reading Foundation 3.0 - 4.5
courses.description: Khóa học cung cấp kiến thức nền tảng cho kỹ năng IELTS Reading, phù hợp với học viên trình độ Beginner. Nội dung tập trung vào cách đọc hiểu bài IELTS, nhận diện từ khóa, skimming, scanning, xử lý câu hỏi True/False/Not Given, Matching, Multiple Choice và Gap Fill. Sau khóa học, học viên có thể nắm vững phương pháp làm bài cơ bản và cải thiện tốc độ đọc hiểu.

courses.title: IELTS Speaking: Band 7.5+ Masterclass
courses.description: Succeed in your IELTS Speaking test with our advanced masterclass. Focuses on fluency, pronunciation, range of vocabulary, and coherent structuring of Parts 1, 2, and 3.

courses.title: IELTS Writing Task 1: Academic Report Guide
courses.description: Learn how to summarize and describe visual information (bar charts, line graphs, pie charts, tables, diagrams, maps) systematically to score Band 7.0+ in Task 1.

courses.title: IELTS Listening: High-Score Strategies
courses.description: Develop your active listening skills and build strategies for all 4 sections. Learn how to handle distractors, identify key synonyms, and complete sentences accurately.

courses.title: IELTS Reading: Speed & Accuracy Booster
courses.description: Tackle long academic texts with confidence. Master time management strategies, learn skimming and scanning, and avoid traps in True/False/Not Given and List of Headings.
syllabus: Deconstruct True, False, Not Given Questions

freeResources.title: The language clinic: Live Q&A session
freeResources.description: Join our live event and ask our expert educator your questions about the English language and learning English.

passages.title: Passage 1 - Urban Transport
passages.content: Public transport systems shape how people move through modern cities. Reliable buses and trains reduce congestion, lower pollution, and make education and work more accessible. However, service frequency and ticket prices still affect whether residents choose public transport over private vehicles.

task2.prompt: Some people think universities should focus on practical skills, while others believe academic knowledge is more important. Discuss both views and give your opinion.

passages.title: Passage 1 - Sleep and Study
passages.content: Researchers have found that sleep affects concentration, memory, and emotional control. Students who sleep consistently often perform better on tasks that require planning and problem solving than those who study late into the night.

passages.title: Passage 1 - Community Libraries
passages.content: Community libraries now offer more than books. Many provide language clubs, digital training, and quiet study rooms. These services are especially useful for learners who do not have reliable internet access at home.

questions.passage:
THE INDUSTRIAL REVOLUTION

The Industrial Revolution, which began in Britain in the mid-18th century, fundamentally transformed how goods were produced and how people lived. Before this period, most manufacturing was done by hand, often in people's homes or small workshops — a system known as cottage industry. The introduction of machinery powered by steam engines and later electricity dramatically increased production capacity.

Factories emerged as the new centres of production, drawing workers from rural areas into rapidly growing urban centres. Cities such as Manchester, Birmingham, and Leeds expanded at extraordinary rates, often without adequate housing, sanitation, or infrastructure to support the influx of population.

While the revolution brought unprecedented economic growth and new consumer goods, it also created new social problems: difficult working conditions, child labour, and overcrowded, polluted cities became widespread concerns. Social reformers and early trade unions eventually pushed for legislation to address these problems, laying the groundwork for modern labour laws and social welfare systems.

The revolution also had global consequences, enabling Britain to build a vast industrial empire and reshaping trade relationships across continents. The technologies and economic models it generated influenced industrialisation processes in Europe, the United States, and later Asia throughout the 19th and 20th centuries.
questions.prompt: According to the passage, what was the PRIMARY effect of introducing machinery during the Industrial Revolution?
questions.questionText: According to the passage, what was the PRIMARY effect of introducing machinery during the Industrial Revolution?
options: It caused widespread unemployment among craftspeople
It improved working conditions in factories
It led to the development of new energy sources

questions.passage:
THE INDUSTRIAL REVOLUTION

The Industrial Revolution, which began in Britain in the mid-18th century, fundamentally transformed how goods were produced and how people lived. Before this period, most manufacturing was done by hand, often in people's homes or small workshops — a system known as cottage industry. The introduction of machinery powered by steam engines and later electricity dramatically increased production capacity.

Factories emerged as the new centres of production, drawing workers from rural areas into rapidly growing urban centres. Cities such as Manchester, Birmingham, and Leeds expanded at extraordinary rates, often without adequate housing, sanitation, or infrastructure to support the influx of population.

While the revolution brought unprecedented economic growth and new consumer goods, it also created new social problems: difficult working conditions, child labour, and overcrowded, polluted cities became widespread concerns. Social reformers and early trade unions eventually pushed for legislation to address these problems, laying the groundwork for modern labour laws and social welfare systems.

The revolution also had global consequences, enabling Britain to build a vast industrial empire and reshaping trade relationships across continents. The technologies and economic models it generated influenced industrialisation processes in Europe, the United States, and later Asia throughout the 19th and 20th centuries.
questions.prompt: Social reformers successfully eliminated child labour during the Industrial Revolution.
questions.questionText: Social reformers successfully eliminated child labour during the Industrial Revolution.

questions.passage:
THE INDUSTRIAL REVOLUTION

The Industrial Revolution, which began in Britain in the mid-18th century, fundamentally transformed how goods were produced and how people lived. Before this period, most manufacturing was done by hand, often in people's homes or small workshops — a system known as cottage industry. The introduction of machinery powered by steam engines and later electricity dramatically increased production capacity.

Factories emerged as the new centres of production, drawing workers from rural areas into rapidly growing urban centres. Cities such as Manchester, Birmingham, and Leeds expanded at extraordinary rates, often without adequate housing, sanitation, or infrastructure to support the influx of population.

While the revolution brought unprecedented economic growth and new consumer goods, it also created new social problems: difficult working conditions, child labour, and overcrowded, polluted cities became widespread concerns. Social reformers and early trade unions eventually pushed for legislation to address these problems, laying the groundwork for modern labour laws and social welfare systems.

The revolution also had global consequences, enabling Britain to build a vast industrial empire and reshaping trade relationships across continents. The technologies and economic models it generated influenced industrialisation processes in Europe, the United States, and later Asia throughout the 19th and 20th centuries.
questions.prompt: Before the Industrial Revolution, manufacturing was primarily carried out in people's homes.
questions.questionText: What is the main purpose of paragraph 1?
What is one benefit of reliable public transport?
questions.prompt: What is one benefit of reliable public transport?
questions.explanation: The passage says reliable buses and trains reduce congestion.

questions.questionText: Ticket prices can influence public transport use.
questions.prompt: Ticket prices can influence public transport use.
questions.explanation: The final sentence mentions ticket prices affect whether residents choose public transport.

questions.questionText: What should the student confirm first?
questions.prompt: What should the student confirm first?

questions.questionText: What does sleep affect according to the passage?
questions.prompt: What does sleep affect according to the passage?

categories.name: Environment
categories.description: IELTS vocabulary for climate, ecology, and sustainability topics.

categories.name: Technology
categories.description: IELTS vocabulary for innovation, digital life, and automation topics.

categories.name: Education
categories.description: IELTS vocabulary for learning, universities, and academic development.

flashcards.meaning: Trở nên tệ hơn, suy giảm
flashcards.example: The political situation in the region has deteriorated rapidly.
The changes to the national health system will be implemented next year.
There was a substantial increase in the number of applications.
These diseases are more prevalent among young children.
Action is required to mitigate the effect of global warming.

flashcards.meaning: Nạn phá rừng, sự tàn phá rừng
flashcards.example: Deforestation is destroying large areas of tropical rain forest.
The mining project threatens one of the world’s richest areas of biodiversity.

flashcards.meaning: Sự thải ra, khí thải (độc hại)
flashcards.example: The government has pledged to cut carbon emissions by 20%.

flashcards.meaning: Bền vững, không gây hại môi trường
flashcards.example: An ecologically sustainable farming system is needed.
The panda is threatened by the disappearance of its natural habitat.

flashcards.meaning: Cung cấp chỗ ở, đáp ứng
flashcards.example: The hotel can accommodate up to 500 guests.
Never leave your luggage unattended.

flashcards.meaning: Vô cùng thú vị, hấp dẫn
flashcards.example: It’s a fascinating city with a lot of history.
You don’t seem very enthusiastic about the party.
Upbringing plays an important part in determining a person’s character.
Don’t bring up the subject of money.
The match was called off because of bad weather.
I am looking forward to meeting you.
Never put off until tomorrow what you can do today.
He turned down the job offer.
I go to the cinema once in a blue moon.
The exam was a piece of cake.
I’m feeling a bit under the weather today.

flashcards.meaning: Phá vỡ sự ngại ngùng (khi mới gặp)
flashcards.example: Jim told a joke to break the ice.

flashcards.word: Cost an arm and a leg
flashcards.example: This car cost an arm and a leg.
His role has always been ambiguous.
He stood staring at the dead body, unable to comprehend.
All evening her husband contradicted everything she said.
Our research attempts to evaluate the effectiveness of the different drugs.
The new car design incorporates all the latest safety features.
They promised to legislate against cigarette advertising.
The war was a paradigm of the destructive side of human nature.
There are qualitative differences between the two products.
He supplements his income by working in a bar at night.
Do you have a valid passport?
Pollution can have disastrous effects on the delicately balanced ecosystem.
Energy conservation reduces your fuel bills and helps the environment.
There is concern that the giant panda will soon become extinct.
Renewable sources of energy such as wind and solar power.
The manifesto includes tough measures to tackle road congestion and environmental pollution.
The effects of global warming are already being felt.
Companies are measuring their carbon footprints.
The sea turtle is an endangered species.
Carbon dioxide is produced by the burning of fossil fuels.
The railway track is heavily overgrown with vegetation.
It gives you an unfair advantage.
What a brilliant idea!
We celebrated our 25th wedding anniversary in Florence.
Our luggage was checked all the way through to our final destination.
The food was excellent.
He is a frequent visitor to this country.
We cannot guarantee that our flights will never be delayed.
She hesitated before replying.
The actors inspired the kids with their enthusiasm.
They went on a long train journey across India.
She didn’t give up work when she had the baby.
Who’s going to look after the children while you’re away?
She wants to repay the committee for the help she received when setting up in business.
The plane took off an hour late.

flashcards.meaning: Tìm ra giải pháp, tập thể dục
flashcards.example: I work out regularly to keep fit.
What brought about the change in his attitude?
Extensive tests have been carried out on the patient.
We found out later that we had been at the same school.
Please go on with what you’re doing and don’t let us interrupt you.

flashcards.meaning: Bịa đặt, trang điểm, tạo thành
flashcards.example: He made up some excuse about his daughter being sick.
I hate going to the dentist, but I’ll just have to bite the bullet.
After 14 hours of working, we decided to call it a day.

flashcards.word: Hit the nail on the head
flashcards.meaning: Nói trúng phóc, hoàn toàn chính xác
flashcards.example: You hit the nail on the head there, David.

flashcards.word: Let the cat out of the bag
flashcards.meaning: Vô tình tiết lộ bí mật
flashcards.pronunciation: /let ðə kæt aʊt əv ðə bæɡ/
flashcards.example: I wanted it to be a surprise, but my sister let the cat out of the bag.

flashcards.meaning: Tiết lộ sự thật, bí mật
flashcards.example: Come on, spill the beans! What did he say?

flashcards.word: Take with a grain of salt
flashcards.example: You have to take everything she says with a grain of salt.
She has stuck with me through thick and thin.
I’m feeling a bit under the weather today.

flashcards.meaning: Hiểu được điều gì đó phức tạp
flashcards.example: I just couldn’t wrap my head around what had happened.

flashcards.meaning: Trong cái rủi có cái may
flashcards.example: Losing that job was a blessing in disguise really.
